Insight: DAM prices peak in May (avg ₹5.80/kWh) and reach their lowest in Aug (avg ₹3.43/kWh). Scheduling generation maintenance during trough months minimises revenue loss.

The top 3 revenue months account for 12.7% of total annual market revenue. High DAM prices during summer months (April–June) align with Khavda's peak solar irradiance, creating a favourable double-upside for market participation.
The highest single-day market revenue of ₹15.27 Lakhs was recorded on 16 May 2025, when generation reached 127.2 MW at a DAM clearing price of ₹12.00/kWh.

IEX DAM price volatility stands at 27.6%, indicating significant market price risk. Implementing Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs) for 60–70% of generation capacity is recommended to hedge against spot market price swings.

With a market price floor of ₹2.05/kWh observed, Khavda's variable generation cost remains well below this threshold, ensuring positive contribution margin under all IEX market clearing scenarios. The average price of ₹4.43/kWh provides a strong margin above typical renewable LCOE of ₹1.80–2.20/kWh.
